response.justification: Bogdan is a credible staff-level IC with a genuine breadth of distributed systems experience and current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work that maps well to the role's AI-native environment. However, Python — a stated must-have — is entirely absent from both the skills section and every role description, which is a material gap at this level. Additionally, the resume provides little evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, formal incident response, or on-call ownership, and the most directly relevant high-throughput systems work (Dunnhumby, millions of requests/sec) is now six years old. The current re:cinq engagement is recent and compelling but too short to fully derisk the seniority claims for a staff-level hire.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq involves building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks, directly matching the AI-native engineering environment the role targets

Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby — micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O — maps to the core technical requirement
Consistent staff-level IC ownership across multiple companies, including open-source platform stewardship (GISCollective) and technical community contributions (workshops, talks)
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume's skills list and all role descriptions despite being listed as a must-have technical skill for this position
No documented evidence of cross-functional stakeholder management, formal on-call rotations, or incident-response ownership at any role

High-throughput distributed systems work is concentrated at Dunnhumby (ended January 2020); the five subsequent years skew toward TypeScript product work and tooling rather than large-scale infrastructure
